topic: This Quarter We Choose Bold — a motivational closer for Canopy Inc.'s Q4 all-hands rallying 340 employees around a single product moonshot: CanopyAI.

topic: The Question That Closes Every Deal — a motivational sales kickoff presentation arguing that discovering the prospect's real pain point—not pitching features—is the only close that matters.

Before RelayCRM: Spreadsheets, Email Chains, Chaos

topic: A Roof Over Ruby: Building Detroit's Youth Shelter — an ignition-style talk raising funds to convert the abandoned Ruby Theatre into a 60-bed youth shelter by winter 2025.
Screen-Free Classrooms: The Finland Model — a brief arguing that banning screens for children under 12 in schools, modeled on Finland's 2023 EdTech policy, dramatically improves literacy outcomes.
outline_hint: Our Children Can't Read
Screen Time in Classrooms Has Tripled Since 2019
Finland Did the Opposite
In 2023: Screens Banned for Ages 6–12
Result: Literacy Rates Rose 14% in Two Years
The Finland Model: Analog First, Digital Later
What This Looks Like in Our State
Objection: 'We Need Digital Skills' — Reply: 'After Age 12, Yes'
The Bill Is Drafted. We Need Your Vote.
Screen-Free. Literacy-First.

We Count Your Impact

Join Nuvola. Work Like an Adult.

topic: Pay Yourself First. Always. — a manifesto-style primer teaching freelancers that automating savings before any other expense is the single behavior that separates financial stability from chaos.

topic: The Letters That Stopped a War: The Cuban Missile Crisis Hotline — a 7-minute lecture on how a single teletype message from Khrushchev to Kennedy on October 26, 1962 de-escalated nuclear standoff.

'If It Isn't a Clear Yes, It's a No'

topic: The 30-Day Sketch Habit: One Pen, One Notebook — a creative manifesto arguing that artistic skill comes from daily volume, not occasional inspiration, using the 30-day challenge format.

Day 1–10: Draw What's in Front of You
Day 11–20: Draw Faster. Don't Erase.
Day 21–30: Draw What Doesn't Exist Yet

topic: The Linden Garden Collective: Dirt, Seeds, Neighbors — a community update on converting a vacant lot into 20 raised beds, told through three seasons and one shared harvest.

The Scientific Method in Six Words: Observe, Question, Hypothesize, Test, Analyze, Share — a 9th-grade biology lesson where each step gets its own emphatic slide with one example from Mendel's pea experiments.
outline_hint: Science Is a Verb, Not a Noun
Step 1: OBSERVE — Mendel Noticed Peas Differed in Color
Step 2: QUESTION — Why Are Some Peas Green and Others Yellow?
Step 3: HYPOTHESIZE — 'A Single Factor Determines Color'
Step 4: TEST — Cross-Pollinate and Count the Offspring
Step 5: ANALYZE — The 3:1 Ratio Changed Biology
Step 6: SHARE — Mendel Published. The World Ignored Him. For 35 Years.
Six Words. One Method. Every Discovery.
Your Turn: Observe Something in This Room
Now Question It.

topic: Ikigai: The Intersection Where Life Makes Sense — a cultural explainer cutting through Western misinterpretation to present ikigai as the simple Okinawan concept of 'a reason to get up in the morning.'
Chantal Akerman and the Radical Power of Stillness — a 10-minute film essay arguing that Akerman's static camera in 'Jeanne Dielman' forces audiences to confront the invisible labor of women.
outline_hint: The Longest Three Hours in Cinema
Stanley Kubrick Called It 'Boring.' He Missed the Point.
Static Camera = Refusal to Look Away
Scene 1: Peeling Potatoes — 5 Unbroken Minutes
The Kitchen Is the Battlefield
When the Camera Finally Moves, Everything Has Changed
Invisible Labor Made Visible
Akerman Was 25. She Changed Film Forever.
Stillness Is Not Emptiness.

Choose Depth.

topic: Serverless Is a Scam (And What Comes Next) — a lightning talk arguing that serverless computing hides operational complexity instead of eliminating it, and proposing 'minimal-server' as the real answer.

You Still Pay for Cold Starts
You Still Debug What You Can't See
You Still Vendor-Lock Your Architecture
The Real Problem: Operational Complexity, Not Server Count
Minimal-Server: One Process. One Container. Full Control.
Example: A 20ms Cold Start vs. a 200ms Lambda
Own Your Stack. Shrink Your Stack.

topic: Clean Water for Kayanga Village: One Well. 800 Lives. — an ignition-style fundraising talk for drilling a single borehole well in Kayanga, Tanzania, with a $12,000 ask and a 90-day timeline.

Touch First: Why Mobile-First Is Dead and Thumb-First Won — a design review manifesto for the Reva banking app redesign, arguing that reachable thumb zone, not screen size, should drive layout decisions.
outline_hint: Mobile-First Was Right in 2012
It's Wrong Now
Everyone Has a Big Screen. No One Has Long Thumbs.
The Thumb Zone: Bottom 60% Is Home
Current Reva App: Primary Action at Top Right. Unreachable.
New Reva App: Primary Action at Bottom Center. Instant.
Three Flows Redesigned. All Thumb-First.
Stop Designing for Screens. Design for Hands.
